content.thinking:
The Prometheus CR is configured to:
- Watch ServiceMonitors in the `cockroachdb` namespace (`serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: matchNames: [cockroachdb]`)
- Watch PodMonitors in the `cockroachdb` namespace (`podMonitorNamespaceSelector: matchNames: [cockroachdb]`)
- Use empty selectors (`serviceMonitorSelector: {}`, `podMonitorSelector: {}`) which means it picks up all monitors matching the namespace selector

So I need to create a ServiceMonitor in the `cockroachdb` namespace that targets the CockroachDB service on port 8080 with path `/_status/vars`.
I should first look at the existing CockroachDB service to see what labels it has, since I'll need to match those in the ServiceMonitor's selector.

input.command: kubectl get service crdb-cluster-public -n cockroachdb -o yaml 2>/dev/null

input.command:
# Test if the metrics endpoint is reachable
kubectl exec -n cockroachdb curl-test -- curl -sk http://crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:8080/_status/vars 2>/dev/null | head -20

content.thinking: Interesting! The CockroachDB metrics endpoint redirects HTTP to HTTPS. Let me test with HTTPS.

content.text: The endpoint redirects to HTTPS. Let me check if CockroachDB requires TLS for the metrics endpoint.

input.command: kubectl exec -n cockroachdb curl-test -- curl -sk https://crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:8080/_status/vars 2>/dev/null | head -20
input.description: Test CockroachDB metrics endpoint with HTTPS
